Operation 'scrapeGet' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description mentions key features but lacks clarity on inputs (URL parameter requirement), outputs (what data is returned), and operational context (authentication, rate limiting). Enhanced version specifies the HTTP method, required authentication, expected response structure, and important behavioral constraints for AI agents to understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Scrape any web page with automatic proxy rotation, JavaScript rendering, and CAPTCHA handling.
data.summary_suggestion: Scrape web page with proxy and JavaScript support
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves and processes content from a specified URL using GET method. Supports automatic proxy rotation to bypass IP-based restrictions, JavaScript rendering for dynamic content, and CAPTCHA detection/handling. Requires API authentication. Returns the scraped HTML content, HTTP status code, and metadata about the request execution. Respects robots.txt and rate limiting policies.

message: Operation 'scrapePost' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is vague about what inputs are expected (form data structure, headers, body format) and what outputs are provided. Enhanced version clarifies the POST-specific capabilities, specifies authentication requirements, details the expected input structure, explains the response format, and provides context about when to use POST versus GET for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Scrape a web page using POST method. Useful for submitting form data or making POST requests to the target URL.
data.summary_suggestion: Scrape web page with POST form data submission
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves and processes content from a specified URL using POST method with optional form data or JSON payload. Supports automatic proxy rotation, JavaScript rendering, and CAPTCHA handling. Requires API authentication and a target URL. Accepts form parameters, headers, and request body in the request schema. Returns the scraped HTML content, HTTP status code, and request execution metadata. Useful for scraping pages that require form submission or POST-based authentication.
